content: Hey, I'm so excited for the weekend. I've been working non-stop as a yoga instructor and wellness coach all week.
I'm glad you're looking forward to the weekend! What do you have planned?
I'm actually planning to take my dog, Willow, on a hike on Saturday. We adopted her from a shelter a few months ago and she's been such a great addition to our family.
That's so great! I'm sure Willow loves spending time with you. What made you decide to adopt a dog?
My partner, Alex, and I had been thinking about getting a dog for a while, and we finally decided to take the plunge. It's been a great decision - Willow brings so much joy to our lives.
That's wonderful. It's great that you've found a furry companion to share your life with. Do you have any fun plans for the spring?
Actually, I'm planning to run a marathon on April 29th, which I've been training for for months. I'm a bit nervous, but I'm excited to see how I do.
Good luck with the marathon! That's a huge accomplishment. What's your strategy going into the race?
I'm planning to focus on my pacing and try to stay hydrated throughout the race. I've also been listening to a lot of podcasts to distract myself during my long runs.
That's a great way to stay entertained during your runs. What kind of podcasts do you like to listen to?
I'm really into comedy podcasts, especially stand-up comedy. It's a great way to lift my mood and take my mind off the run.
That's a great way to stay positive and motivated. Do you have any other hobbies or interests that you enjoy in your free time?
I'm also really into vintage cars, which I've been restoring in my spare time. It's a fun hobby that allows me to be creative and work with my hands.
That's really cool! Restoring vintage cars can be a great way to relax and express yourself. Have you ever taken any of your restored cars on a road trip?
Actually, Alex and I are planning to take a road trip to the Pacific Coast Highway on May 20th in my restored 1969 Mustang. We're really looking forward to it.
That sounds like an amazing trip! The Pacific Coast Highway is one of the most scenic drives in the country.
Definitely. I've heard great things about the views and the stops along the way. Okay, I should probably get going, but it was great chatting with you!
You too, Hayden! Good luck with your marathon and your road trip!
